title: UnitedHealthcare exec talks AI, affordability and member engagement
summary: Dan Kueter, CEO of UnitedHealthcare Employer & Individual, joins the podcast to talk about what employers are asking for in today’s commercial insurance market and how UnitedHealthcare is using digital tools, AI and more personalized benefit design to support member engagement.

title: Jeff Bezos’s Prometheus raises $12B to build an ‘artificial general engineer’ for the physical world
summary: The new round values the physical AI startup that aims to automate heavy engineering and drug design at $41 billion.

title: AI use is surging across HHS, jumping 148% at the FDA in 2025, Bipartisan Policy Center data finds
summary: There was a big uptick in the use of AI across all HHS agencies in 2025, though analysts note some use cases may “reflect new reporting requirements instead of new deployments."

title: Health plans say AI is pushing healthcare costs higher
summary: Nearly 70% of surveyed health plans said providers’ use of AI documentation and coding tools was a top three trend inflating commercial healthcare costs next year, according to a new report from PwC.

title: Humana divests interest in end-of-life provider Gentiva valued at $900M
summary: The insurer is offloading its interest in the massive end-of-life care provider to an undisclosed group of investors, amid widespread concern about private investors’ activity in healthcare.

title: UPMC to lay off 200 employees, cut 300 open positions
summary: A spokesperson said the layoffs were primarily in non-clinical or member facing roles.

title: Medicare insolvency date creeps forward thanks to ‘Big Beautiful Bill,’ trustees find
summary: The trust fund underpinning Medicare’s hospital benefit is set to run out of money one quarter earlier than previously expected as tax cuts in the GOP’s reconciliation legislation shrink Medicare’s revenue.

title: Drug Sites Hijacked Spotify’s Search Ranking Through Fake Podcasts
summary: A joint congressional report describes a spam operation that turned tens of thousands of fake podcasts into search-engine bait for illegal pharmacy and scam sites.

title: AHIP 2026: Why Ascendiun CEO Paul Markovich is bullish on building out a digital health record for patients
summary: LAS VEGAS — If Ascendiun CEO Paul Markovich could eliminate one buzzword from the healthcare dictionary, it would be "interoperability."

title: Hospital associations push CMS for higher 2027 pay bump, softer ramp-up for mandatory model
summary: Public comments submitted this week rule skewered a proposed 2.4% net rate increase as insufficient in light of substantial financial headwinds. An accompanying mandatory expansion of a tested episode-based payment model, the CJR-X Model, was also flatly opposed.

title: Job titles of the future: Nature’s drug designer

title: Virtual care tech companies launch 'out-of-the-box' RPM tool for pharmacies
summary: The program's goal is to empower pharmacies, particularly independent ones in rural areas, to oversee and support chronic care patients between scheduled office visits.

title: OIG: Frequent MA prior authorization denials for long-term care hospitals, inpatient rehab
summary: A new federal watchdog report dings the three largest Medicare Advantage insurers for frequent prior authorization denials for long-term care hospital and inpatient rehabilitation stays. Another found that initial denials among skilled-nursing facilities were near-unanimously...

title: Provider groups file lawsuit against HHS over anti-trans Ryan White funding rules
summary: The new funding requirements mandate recipients of Ryan White funds to not acknowledge the identities of trans patients or use funds for gender-affirming care.

title: UHS deal exposes pitfalls of physician group acquisitions
summary: Despite major financial struggles, some providers at a physician group in Washington, D.C. say their new — and much larger — parent is not offering the lifeline they want.

title: David Sinclair plans to test whole-body rejuvenation drugs in the XPrize competition
summary: The outspoken longevity scientist David Sinclair has been predicting that one day, you’ll go to the doctor and get a prescription that will make you 10 years younger. Now MIT Technology Review has learned that he has plans to launch human tests of an oral “reprogramming” drug...

title: PSG report examines payers and employers’ attitudes towards GLP-1 coverage, unbundling’ PBM models
summary: The annual drug benefit design report drew insights from 237 benefits leaders across employers, health plans and unions.

title: CalOptima Health approves $430M rate increase to keep safety-net providers afloat
summary: The Orange County Medi-Cal plan said it would tap its reserves to support hospitals and specialists whose financial stability is threatened by Medicaid funding and eligibility changes from the One Big Beautiful Bill Act.

title: Vega Health licenses AI models from Parkland Center for Clinical Innovation to predict patient risks
summary: Vega helps customers with testing, fine-tuning and deployment of AI. Since 2019, PCCI's models have identified nearly 3 million at-risk individuals.

title: Americans mostly blame insurers for rising healthcare costs, survey finds
summary: AHIP argued the survey, which was commissioned by a pro-hospital advocacy group, is a blatant attempt by hospitals to deflect the blame for ever-higher spending.

title: Hundreds of hospitals warned over price transparency failings, AP reports
summary: At least 519 hospitals nationwide have received a warning notice or Corrective Action Plan request due to price transparency deficiencies since April, according to a "senior administration official" cited by the AP.

title: Abridge picks up strategic investment from Eli Lilly, expands payer, research workflows
